summary.rationale: Positive employee sentiment; moderate innovation; 40 reviews

reviews.title: Very good.
reviews.pros: Pay, coworkers, overall nothing to complain about.
reviews.cons: None that I can think of.

reviews.title: Product Manager
reviews.pros:
- Led end-to-end product development of an AI-driven embedded finance platform, enabling partner applications to deliver real-time credit decisions via APIs — reducing loan approval time from days to under 5 seconds.
- Defined product vision, strategy, and roadmap aligned with business objectives; collaborated with engineering, data science, and compliance teams to ensure innovation within FCRA, ECOA, and GDPR frameworks.
- Partnered with data scientists to design and deploy an explainable ML-based credit-scoring model, achieving 94% predictive accuracy and transparent outputs using SHAP-based interpretability.
- Managed a backlog of 40+ features, prioritized initiatives using the RICE framework, and delivered the MVP 20% ahead of schedule while maintaining enterprise-grade reliability and uptime.
- Launched a self-service partner dashboard enabling instant API integration, credit analytics, and performance monitoring — improving partner onboarding efficiency by 30%.
- Established measurable success metrics and continuous feedback loops, tracking KPIs such as approval-rate lift (+15%), model drift, and partner retention to drive iterative product optimization.
reviews.cons:
- Led end-to-end product development of an AI-driven embedded finance platform, enabling partner applications to deliver real-time credit decisions via APIs — reducing loan approval time from days to under 5 seconds.
- Defined product vision, strategy, and roadmap aligned with business objectives; collaborated with engineering, data science, and compliance teams to ensure innovation within FCRA, ECOA, and GDPR frameworks.
- Partnered with data scientists to design and deploy an explainable ML-based credit-scoring model, achieving 94% predictive accuracy and transparent outputs using SHAP-based interpretability.
- Managed a backlog of 40+ features, prioritized initiatives using the RICE framework, and delivered the MVP 20% ahead of schedule while maintaining enterprise-grade reliability and uptime.
- Launched a self-service partner dashboard enabling instant API integration, credit analytics, and performance monitoring — improving partner onboarding efficiency by 30%.

reviews.title: Great Experience But Frequent Layoffs
reviews.pros: JPM is a great place to work, with many opportunities and different lines of business to explore for career growth. You’re surrounded by many talented people.
reviews.cons: Recently, JPM has had layoffs and outsourced some roles to India and other parts of Asia. Additionally, advancements in AI technology are impacting certain positions.

reviews.title: Excellent Staff Culture
reviews.pros: Great place to work and great company culture
reviews.cons: Work life balance could be better improved
reviews.advice: Keep doing what you are doing

reviews.title: Great job
reviews.pros: It was a great company to work for
reviews.cons: Worked long hours with some demanding clients

reviews.title: Planning is more of a marketing initiative than a strategic initiative
reviews.pros: Guaranteed pay for a period of time and good benefits
reviews.cons: The firm is stuck to the old ways. They say that financial planning is a top priority, but use a sub-par proprietary planning software. If you actually want to be a planner, not just in investment sales, you better have good, experienced bankers that can identify those prospects.
reviews.advice: Align banker, branch manager, and advisor compensation.

reviews.title: Great place
reviews.pros: Its good. I would say a challenging place if you want it to be, they have a lot of problems that need to be solved and you will get proper compensation in bonus.
reviews.cons: Nothing much, a lot of airheads / extra fluff people here. Usually the older crowd that is kinda floating through.

reviews.title: Great career opportunity
reviews.pros: The best place to start your career and learn with the brightest people
reviews.cons: High intensity and pace of the business

reviews.title: This place sucks and everyone knows it
reviews.pros:
You get a paycheck
Some cool projects
Not as demanding as agencies or consultant firms in some ways
reviews.cons:
Dumb product people make decisions but have no idea what design is and just do what the business people tell them
No process or direction yet higher ups expect you to follow them
Five day RTTO even though most of your meetings and teammates are in another state
You're either replacing people with AI or you're the ones being replaced by AI
Toxic culture, minor complaints or feedback can blow up into out of proportion panic for no reason
Constant Micromanagement. Its not enough to do your job or even do your job well. I get that perception matters but here it seems like perception is all that matters.
reviews.advice:
Fire them. Fire yourselves.

I don't know anyone that is happy here. I know that is kind of cliche but honestly when I listened to other people telling me about how much they hate it here, I never felt the same way. Now I do. It's not the worst place I've ever worked at but it is getting there.

reviews.title: Depends on your team and market assignment
reviews.pros:
- Obtainable entry-level salary job with good benefits, good company, and flexibility in your day
- Great learning experience about banking, credit, mortgages,
- Team-oriented atmosphere
reviews.cons:
- The entire day is spent sitting at a desk answering phones and responding to emails
- Depending on your market, the workload can be quite heavy or light, and it is all by chance

reviews.title: Pros and cons
reviews.pros:
Great benefit payments
Great vacation packages (4 weeks)
Great team that priorities team building
Good work balance
reviews.cons:
5 days in office 
Expensive cafeteria food

reviews.title: Not bad
reviews.pros: Steady, consistent overall balance throughout the firm
reviews.cons: Wish there was more clarity in expectations and wish there was less politics about yearly reviews

reviews.title: High volume call center
reviews.pros:
Benefits
Work from home or hybrid
reviews.cons:
• Low Pay for job expected to do
• Micromanagers
• Poor training
• Unrealistic metrics 
• Difficult and stressful calls requiring you to use multiple systems 
• no work or life balance 
• heavy workload 5x the workload on 1 person
• high turnover
reviews.advice: Management needs to advocate for the agents, higher pay for agents, hire more agents and don’t expect the agents to keep doing overtime.

reviews.title: A great challenge
reviews.pros: Lots of opportunities for growth and internal mobility. Very intelligent people.
reviews.cons: Little to no work life balance, and the culture follows that which Jamie sets. Employees feel surveilled and distrusted.
reviews.advice: Consider tangible efforts to at least measure employee wellness.

reviews.title: Pretty good company and lack of benefit
reviews.pros: A good reputation for financial industry
reviews.cons: a lot of political fighting in the product areas

reviews.title: Good place but same story
reviews.pros: Decent pay, coworkers are great, management is good at my specific location although heard bad things at other locations.
reviews.cons: Call center so focus is always on output and service levels. Asked multiple times to develop career but always same story, service levels priority.
reviews.advice: Too worried about data and "showing number" for what employees are asking for. No focus on showing values in action but instead focusing on proving that whats being asked for is beneficial.

reviews.title: 10/10 terrible place to work
reviews.pros: There are no pros to working here
reviews.cons: They don't give any raises and constantly expect you to learn new skills and do more and more things for the same job title and pay. My Dr recommended disability accomodation was denied by them because they "recognize it as a disability but don't recognize that I should WFH for it" they are obsessed with being in the office for no reason. They hate WFH because Jamie Dimon threw a fit about zoom. They offer Guild education incentives that go nowhere and keep you in your current role. There is no mobility. The management is terrible. They do not hire anyone that deserves it and don't fire anyone that deserves it. At my previous role we had enforced overtime that if you missed it you could be fired for. They paid everyone the same whether you had a degree or not. There is zero flexibility for anything. And they boast about how they give so much PTO but wont let you work remotely or have the day off if there is an extreme weather event. Multiple times told us we were expected to drive into work during tornado/hail storms when I had the capability to work remotely. They also enforced company wide rto when they didn't have enough space and now plan to expand their site. This would not matter if they just hired people remotely. Covid taught us there are no need for workplaces anymore. Red flags everywhere, do not work here.
reviews.advice: Become flexible. WFH is not the enemy. Get a new CEO.

reviews.title: Manager Support Needed
reviews.pros: - Commission is good depending on how many deal the FA closes for you
reviews.cons: - A supportive team is imperative for your success. If you don’t have a good branch manager or FA, it’ll show in your commission.

reviews.title: No Offer
reviews.pros: Fast process did not need to talk to people
reviews.cons: No offer, backed out at the end

reviews.title: loan officer assitant
reviews.pros: amazing team to work with so much growth
reviews.cons: need higher base pay for assistants

reviews.title: Solid experience as Executive Director
reviews.pros: Autonomy, organizational culture, benefits, fellow employees, management
reviews.cons: Frequent reductions-in-force, siloes across departments

reviews.title: Summer intern
reviews.pros: Transparency is good. Pay is decent for intern.
reviews.cons: Doesn't look best for full time

reviews.title: good
reviews.pros: good work life balance. flexibility
reviews.cons: pay raise seems lagging to tech firms

reviews.title: Great pay
reviews.pros: Great pay and easy job
reviews.cons: No cons about this job

reviews.title: Depends on what side you are on..
reviews.pros: Hybrid. work life balance for the week
reviews.cons: Multiple issues, Turnover is high, training can leave you behind, cooperate world would let you go for no reason

reviews.title: Strong learning environment with solid work culture
reviews.pros: Supportive team environment, good exposure to modern technologies, structured processes, collaborative culture, and opportunities for professional growth
reviews.cons: Workload can be demanding at times, tight deadlines during peak releases, limited flexibility in some projects, and slower decision-making due to multiple approvals.

reviews.title: not rewarding
reviews.pros: Great health benefits, flexible, easy task, SLA task easily achievable.
reviews.cons: no room to grow, can't apply to certain roles even if you are 100% qualified . (can't skip job levels), favoritism, candidates are already chosen for lateral positions before other employees even get a chance to interview
reviews.advice: stop wasting other people time with interviews when you already made up your mind before accepting applications.

reviews.title: Sick culture
reviews.pros:
1. Competitive compensation package with strong perks and benefits.
2. A mix of highly talented individuals alongside some challenging personalities.
reviews.cons: A toxic culture has negatively impacted team dynamics, while heavy regulatory processes slow progress across the organization and with external partners.
reviews.advice: Time is limited, and lasting value comes from how we treat people and build the organization, not what we accumulate whether your mortgage or pension.

reviews.title: Good place to work, but lacks emphasis on employee benefits
reviews.pros: Good work-life balance, decent compensation, interesting work
reviews.cons: The firm has transitioned to a fully in-person format, so there is no option for hybrid work.

reviews.title: Summer Intern
reviews.pros: Great exposure to real-world finance and a fast-paced, professional environment. The team was supportive, and interns were given meaningful work rather than just administrative tasks. Strong learning opportunities and solid training resources.
reviews.cons: Long hours at times and a steep learning curve, especially for those without prior industry experience.

reviews.title: banker in the branch
reviews.pros: great bonus if you put in the work
reviews.cons: lower tier in the hourly wage have to make your money off bonus and moving up is really hard

reviews.title: Good learning but long hours
reviews.pros: Good deal flow, exposure to industry leading clients
reviews.cons: Hours can be quite long

reviews.title: Bureaucratic organization.
reviews.pros: Big organization with a lot of opportunities to move teams.
reviews.cons: Beware of toxic middle management. Depends on team but there are ample of them who can slow down your growth.

reviews.title: jp review
reviews.pros: good community of same aged engineers
reviews.cons: team by team but trying to hard to be bigtech

reviews.title: Very good company
reviews.pros:
Tons of learning opportunities
Ppls are sharp and hard working 
New office is the best in the city
reviews.cons:
Too big to navigate
Fast speed

reviews.title: Toxic culture
reviews.pros: PTO policy is generous, 401k matching program is good if you stay
reviews.cons: toxic management, return to office, not enough space, 401k only matches once a year

reviews.title: Good
reviews.pros: Very good team and exposure and deal flow and experience
reviews.cons: Long hours that you need to keep yourself super alert and focused

reviews.title: Can be great
reviews.pros:
PTO 
Pay
Work from home
reviews.cons: Depends on what manager you get. Getting help can be hard and stressful. Tends to be hard sometimes

reviews.title: Overall Review of JP Morgan
reviews.pros: Great benefits and good pay for the job
reviews.cons: Very mundane and boring work

reviews.title: Meh
reviews.pros: Good and bad. Honestly there is a lot of change coming that will positively impact the organization.
reviews.cons: Too much to say here. I’m exhausted and need out.
